title: Add image upload and multiple servers icon to attack path visualizer

requirements.summary: Add the ability to load and display user-provided images within the attack path diagram canvas
acceptanceCriteria: Users can upload image files (PNG, JPG, JPEG) from their local system
Uploaded images appear as placeable elements on the attack path canvas
Images can be repositioned, resized, and deleted like other diagram elements
Images persist when diagrams are saved and loaded
Image upload interface is accessible from the attack path toolbar

file_operations.description: Add images array to diagram state, add handlers for adding, moving, resizing, and deleting uploaded images. Include methods to convert File objects to ExternalBlob instances from the blob-storage component. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add an image upload button to the toolbar that triggers a file input (accept PNG, JPG, JPEG). When a file is selected, convert it to ExternalBlob using ExternalBlob.fromBytes() with upload progress tracking via withUploadProgress(). Add the uploaded image to the canvas state.

Add rendering logic for uploaded images from state. Images should be displayed at their stored positions with stored dimensions. Implement drag-to-reposition, corner resize handles, and selection/deletion behavior similar to icons. Images should render using ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() for optimal streaming and caching. Handle click events for selection and deletion when eraser tool is active.
Update diagram save operation to include images array in DiagramState. When loading diagrams, populate images from loaded state. Ensure images are cleared when clearing the canvas.

requirements.summary: Add a multiple servers icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar for representing server clusters
acceptanceCriteria: A new 'multiple servers' icon type is available in the attack path toolbar
The icon visually represents multiple servers (e.g., stacked servers or server cluster)
The icon can be dragged onto the canvas like existing icon types
The icon integrates with existing connection, selection, and manipulation features

file_operations.description: Add a new 'multiple-servers' icon type to the toolbar's draggable icon list. Use the generated image at frontend/public/assets/generated/multiple-servers.dim_128x128.png as the icon source. Position it logically near the existing server icon in the toolbar.

Add mapping for 'multiple-servers' iconType to render the image from frontend/public/assets/generated/multiple-servers.dim_128x128.png. Ensure it follows the same drag-and-drop, selection, and connection behavior as existing icon types.
